title: 03-gut_Characteristics_and_Control_of_Combustion_Instabilities_in_a_Swirl-Stabil
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 1]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Prms, average = (Σ(i=1 to N) p'rms,i)/N, where N is number of data points

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters**

*   Thermoacoustic instability frequency: approximately 200 Hz
*   Phase lag for best control: 60-90 deg
*   Peak amplitude reduction of pressure oscillations (high-pressure baseline case): factor of three
*   Velocity fluctuation reduction with control: over 50% (in certain regions)

**Nomenclature**
*   D = combustor inner diameter
*   Patm = atmospheric pressure
*   p' = instantaneous pressure fluctuations
*   Prms = rms of pressure fluctuations
*   Pbaseline = rms pressure fluctuation without control
*   Q' = instantaneous heat flux fluctuations
*   Qrms = rms of heat flux fluctuations
*   Rep = Reynolds number with respect to the primary airflow rate
*   r/R0 = normalized combustor radius
*   Usec = mean axial velocity of the secondary stream
*   z = length along the combustor axis
*   Φ = equivalence ratio

title: 03-gut_Characteristics_and_Control_of_Combustion_Instabilities_in_a_Swirl-Stabil
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   None found

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 1 Schematic of the combustor:**
    *   Components: Combustion Chamber, Pressure Sensor, Heatflux Sensor, Nozzle, Nozzle exit, Secondary Air, Primary Air, Fuel Line.
*   **Fig. 2 Schematic of a) controller and b) DSP:**
    *   **Controller Components:** Combustion Chamber, Pressure Sensor, Digital Signal Processor , Amplifier, Valve, Fuel Injector, Closed Loop Switch, Solid state relay DC60S7, 12 Volt DC Source, Open Loop Function Generator 200 Hz/20 V
    *   **DSP Components:** ADC Read, Filter, Phase Shift, Amplifier, DAC Write.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Heat release: 30-kW
*   Swirl number based on secondary flow swirler: 0.8
*   Liquid fuel pressure: 160 psi (N/m²)
*   Sauter mean diameter of droplets: 5 to 35 µm
*   Fuel flow rate: 0.75 ml/s
*   Primary airflow velocity (Up): 2-110 m/s
*   Primary airflow pressure: 20 atm
*   Pressure line upstream of plenum: 5 atm
*   Secondary air flow rate: 0.283 to 1.7 m³/s
*   Combustion shell length: 0.6 m
*   Combustion shell diameter (D): 0.14 m
*   Pressure Transducer Frequencies: 50 and 5 KHz
*   Locations of sensors along the length: z/D=0.36, 0.55, 0.73, 1.45, and 2.18
*   Circumferential sensor positions: 90 and 180 deg at z/D = 1.45
*   DSP Interface: 32-bit, 40-Mbytes/s I/O
*   Microprocessor: Texas Instrument 32-bit, 60-MHz
*   Function Generator: 200 Hz/20 V

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Systematic error in LDV: 0.8%
*   Precision error (reacting flow, 5000 samples):  less than 1.4% (95% confidence)
*   Precision error reduction with 10,000 samples: down to 1%
*   Precision error for rms of reacting flow velocities: less than 2% (95% confidence)
*   Uncertainty with 10,000 samples: 1.4%
*   Cold-flow condition samples: 3000
*   Cold-flow condition uncertainty (mean velocity): 2% (95% confidence)
*   Cold-flow condition uncertainty (rms velocity): 2.6% (95% confidence)
*   Reacting flowfield particle size: 0.3 um
*   Probe rotation: 90 deg

title: 03-gut_Characteristics_and_Control_of_Combustion_Instabilities_in_a_Swirl-Stabil
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here is the technical content extracted from the gas turbine research page:

1.  Mathematical equations:

    *   Rep = UD/µ
2.  Graphs/charts:

    *   Figure 5. Different p'rms/Patm conditions (and different equivalence ratio conditions) at centerpoint of the combustor exit: a) temperature and b-d) emissions.
    *   a) Stack temp (°C) vs p'rms (Normalized RMS %).
        *   Axes: Stack temp (°C) from 330 to 730; p'rms (Normalized RMS %) from 1.29 to 2.64
    *   b) CO in ppm vs  p'rms (Normalized RMS %).
        *   Axes: CO in ppm from 0 to 4000; p'rms (Normalized RMS %) from 1.29 to 2.64
    *   c) %CO2 vs  p'rms (Normalized RMS %).
        *   Axes: %CO2 from 1 to 11; p'rms (Normalized RMS %) from 1.29 to 2.64
    *   d) NOx in ppm vs  p'rms (Normalized RMS %).
        *   Axes: NOx in ppm from 24 to 84; p'rms (Normalized RMS %) from 1.29 to 2.64

3.  Tables:

    *   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  Diagrams:

    *   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  Key numbers, constants, parameters:

    *   Maximum particle diameter: 0.9 µm
    *   Mean combustor pressure: 1 atm
    *   Re = 6.2617 x 10^4: 32% increase in pressure fluctuations for a 50% decrease in equivalence ratio
    *   Lower limit of equivalence ratio: 0.26
    *   High-pressure oscillation case: Re, = 6.1626 × 10^4 and φ=0.429
    *   Low-pressure oscillation case: Re, = 6.1473 x 10^4 and φ=0.609
    *   High p'rms/Patm (2-3%)
    *   Low p'rms/Patm (nearly 1%)
    *   High pm/Patm (nearly 3%)
    *   NOx levels at φ=0.609 are due to the higher equivalence ratio
    *   Stack temperatures nearly 200% higher at the lower CO levels
    *   NOx levels are as high as 60 ppm at 4.5% O₂ concentration
    *   Normalized pressure oscillations: 2.5%.

*   4% reduction in temperature at 60-deg phase delay.
*   10% reduction in CO2 and a 30% increase in CO at 60 deg phase delay.
*   225 Hz worst control oscillation
*   200 Hz baseline oscillation
*   60 ppm NOx levels corresponding to first baseline case

title: 03-gut_Characteristics_and_Control_of_Combustion_Instabilities_in_a_Swirl-Stabil
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's a breakdown of the technical data extracted from the provided gas turbine research page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   No mathematical equations are explicitly written out in plain text. References are made to pressure and heat flux sensors, which likely involve governing equations, but are not presented.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 14: Emissions vs phase angle (closed loop)**

    *   **a) Stack Temperature vs Phase Angle**
        *   Axes:
            *   y-axis: temp/temp.baseline (dimensionless)
            *   x-axis: Phase angle (deg)
        *   Curve: Oscillatory behavior around 1.0. Maxima around 120 degrees.
        *   Key Values: Oscillates between approximately 0.94 and 1.04.
    *   **b) %CO2 vs Phase Angle**
        *   Axes:
            *   y-axis: %CO2/%CO2, baseline (dimensionless)
            *   x-axis: Phase angle (deg)
        *   Curve: Oscillatory behavior. Peaks around 120 degrees.
        *   Key Values: Varies between approximately 0.95 and 1.1.
    *   **c) NOx vs Phase Angle**
        *   Axes:
            *   y-axis: NOx/NOx, baseline (dimensionless)
            *   x-axis: Phase angle (deg)
        *   Curve: Notable peaks at around 90 and 240 degrees.
        *   Key Values: Varies between approximately 0.6 and 2
    *   **d) Carbon Monoxide vs Phase Angle**
        *   Axes:
            *   y-axis: CO/CO, baseline (dimensionless)
            *   x-axis: Phase angle (deg)
        *   Curve: Similar shape to %CO2 curve
        *   Key Values: Varies between approximately 0.9 and 1.4.
*   **Figure 15: Three Different Radial Locations**

    *   **a) Normalized Stack Temperature**
        *   Axes:
            *   y-axis: Stack Temp/Stack Tempave (dimensionless)
            *   x-axis: r/R0 (dimensionless, radial position normalized)
        *   Curves:
            *   Control: Decreasing from ~1.25 to ~0.8
            *   Uncontrolled: Decreasing from ~1.1 to ~0.7
        *   Key Values: Values are given at r/R0 = 0, 0.5 and 1 for both Control and Uncontrolled curves.
    *   **b) Normalized NOx**
       *   Axes:
            *   y-axis: NOx/NOxave (dimensionless)
            *   x-axis: r/R0 (dimensionless, radial position normalized)
        *   Curves:
            *   Control: Decreasing from ~1.25 to ~0.7 at r/R0 = 1
            *   Uncontrolled: Decreasing from ~0.92 to ~0.8 at r/R0 = 1
        *   Key Values: Values are given at r/R0 = 0, 0.5 and 1 for both Control and Uncontrolled curves.

**3. Tables:**
* No tables are present

**4. Diagrams**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Frequency: 230 Hz, 200 Hz
*   Phase Delay: 6

title: 03-gut_Characteristics_and_Control_of_Combustion_Instabilities_in_a_Swirl-Stabil
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 12]
Here's a summary of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

2.  **Graphs/Charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3.  **Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  **Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

    *   Frequency of dominant instability: 200 Hz
    *   Phase delay for best control: 60-90 degrees
    *   Phase delay for worst control: 240-270 degrees
    *   Reduction of pressure oscillations with control (high NOx case): around 30%
    *   Reduction of NOx levels with control (high NOx case): nearly 40%
    *   Reduction of 200 Hz oscillation with 60-deg phase lag (high-pressure oscillation case): nearly threefold
    *   Reduction of pressure oscillations with control (high-pressure oscillation case): around 50%
    *   Reductions in the axial rms fluctuations with control: greater than 50%

I have listed all key number values as they appeared in the text.
